title: Overloads with a CultureInfo or an IFormatProvider parameter should be preferred
description: When a System.Globalization.CultureInfo or IFormatProvider object is not supplied, the default value that is supplied by the overloaded member might not have the effect that you want in all locales. You should supply culture-specific information according to the following guidelines: If the value will be displayed to the user, use the current culture. See CultureInfo.CurrentCulture.
recommendation: Review and fix according to the rule guidelines.

checks.message: ToString with format string should specify CultureInfo or IFormatProvider for consistent formatting
Parse operations should specify CultureInfo or IFormatProvider for consistent parsing

logic.detection_conditions: Uses regex to detect ToString/Parse calls without culture-specific parameters in assignment statements
logic.triggering_conditions: Assignment nodes with ToString(format) or Parse operations without CultureInfo/IFormatProvider
logic.exclusion_conditions: Excludes test methods, logging, calls that already include culture parameters
never_trigger_on: Calls already including CultureInfo or IFormatProvider
logic.reasoning: Detects ToString() calls with format strings and Parse operations that don't specify culture information in assignment statements. This can lead to inconsistent behavior across different locales and cultures.

should_not_trigger: [Test] public void TestMethod() { string result = date.ToString("yyyy-MM-dd"); }
